record.text: Gemma 4 makes 'thinking' a native runtime feature — stop faking reasoning with prompts. It's a new API contract between your code and the model. #Gemma4 #MetaAI #LargeLanguageModels

external.description: Gemma 4 arrived with the usual numbers, E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B dense, 128K-256K context, but the real shift is quieter: Gemma 4 makes “thinking” a native runtime feature, not a prompt hack. That turns Gemma 4 from just another open model into a new interface contract between your code and the model. TL;DR Gemma 4’s “native thinking” is an API surface: you’re no longer faking reasoning with prompt tricks, you’re orchestrating around a first‑class thinking channel.
external.title: Gemma 4 Native Thinking Is a Real Developer Shift

record.text: AI model collapse is happening — models learning from their own outputs turn errors into infrastructure. Treat data like code now or watch your AI fail. #ChatGPT #MLOps #ResponsibleAI

external.description: If you’ve asked an LLM for a simple command lately and watched it flail through three wrong answers, you’ve already met AI model collapse. The lab name is new, but the pattern isn’t: once a system starts learning mostly from its own outputs, error becomes infrastructure. The argument here is simple: AI model collapse is not a mysterious research phenomenon, it’s what happens when we treat training data like exhaust instead of like code.
external.title: AI Model Collapse Is Happening: Treat Data as Code Now

Image Prompt

A cute fox with soft, rounded features and large expressive eyes sits at a cozy wooden desk in a softly lit study room, deeply focused on advanced mathematical work. The fox wears a small octopus as a hat, the octopus actively assisting—its tentacles gently holding a quill, flipping through scrolls, and pointing at complex spline equations spread across parchment.

The scene is a close-up composition, centered on the fox’s face, the octopus, and the workspace. The fox’s posture shows curiosity and concentration, slightly leaning forward with attentive eyes reflecting the equations. The octopus has a helpful, engaged expression, subtly guiding the process.

The desk is covered with aged scrolls filled with intricate spline equations, curves, and mathematical notation. Small decorative mushrooms sit along the edges of the workspace, adding a soft natural touch without distraction.

Lighting is cool-toned and ambient, dominated by soft blues, purples, and subtle teal highlights. A gentle but defined light source creates glowing edges around the characters, with smooth luminous highlights and soft contrast. The glow reflects off the parchment and the fox’s eyes, emphasizing clarity and focus.

Textures are clean, polished, and digitally refined—smooth surfaces, no grain, no painterly effect. Colors are vivid but controlled, avoiding oversaturation and yellow tones.

Background remains softly blurred: a cozy study interior with hints of bookshelves and wooden textures, maintaining a calm, intimate atmosphere without visual clutter.

Style: fantasy illustration, clean rounded character design, expressive through posture and shape, cinematic soft lighting, subtle glow, high clarity, emotionally warm but visually cool-toned.
